qtype: Currency conversion, percentage change, ratio problem solving
tex:
**[2018 Oct/Nov, 0580/42, Question 1]**

(a) The Muller family are on holiday in New Zealand.
\newline

(i) (b) They change some euros (€) and receive \$1962 (New Zealand dollars).
\newline
The exchange rate is €1 = \$1.635.
\newline
Calculate the number of euros they change.
\newline

€ 

(c) The family spend 15\% of their New Zealand dollars on a tour.
\newline
Calculate the number of dollars they have left.
\newline

\$ 

(d) The family visit two waterfalls, the Humboldt Falls and the Bridal Veil Falls.
\newline
The ratio of the heights Humboldt Falls : Bridal Veil Falls = 5 : 1.
\newline
The Humboldt Falls are 220 m higher than the Bridal Veil Falls.
\newline
Calculate the height of the Humboldt Falls.
\newline

 m

(e) Water flows over the Browne Falls at a rate of 3680 litres per second.
\newline
After rain, this rate increases to 9752 litres per second.
\newline
Calculate the percentage increase in this rate.
\newline

(f) After rain, water flows over the Sutherland Falls at a rate of 74240 litres per second.
\newline
This is an increase of 45\% on the rate before the rain.
\newline
Calculate the rate before the rain.
\newline

 litres/second

content.content:
The Muller family are on holiday in New Zealand.
\newline

They change some euros (€) and receive \$1962 (New Zealand dollars).
\newline
The exchange rate is €1 = \$1.635.
\newline
Calculate the number of euros they change.
\newline

€ \AnswerLine[][]

The family spend 15\% of their New Zealand dollars on a tour.
\newline
Calculate the number of dollars they have left.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][]

The family visit two waterfalls, the Humboldt Falls and the Bridal Veil Falls.
\newline
The ratio of the heights Humboldt Falls : Bridal Veil Falls = 5 : 1.
\newline
The Humboldt Falls are 220 m higher than the Bridal Veil Falls.
\newline
Calculate the height of the Humboldt Falls.
\newline

Water flows over the Browne Falls at a rate of 3680 litres per second.
\newline
After rain, this rate increases to 9752 litres per second.
\newline
Calculate the percentage increase in this rate.
\newline

After rain, water flows over the Sutherland Falls at a rate of 74240 litres per second.
\newline
This is an increase of 45\% on the rate before the rain.
\newline
Calculate the rate before the rain.
\newline

_rawBody:
\noindent\textbf{[2018 Oct/Nov, 0580/42, Question 1]}\par

\begin{parts}
\item The Muller family are on holiday in New Zealand.

\begin{subparts}
\item They change some euros (€) and receive \$1962 (New Zealand dollars).
The exchange rate is €1 = \$1.635.
Calculate the number of euros they change.

€ \AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item The family spend 15\% of their New Zealand dollars on a tour.
Calculate the number of dollars they have left.

\$ \AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\item The family visit two waterfalls, the Humboldt Falls and the Bridal Veil Falls.
The ratio of the heights Humboldt Falls : Bridal Veil Falls = 5 : 1.
The Humboldt Falls are 220 m higher than the Bridal Veil Falls.
Calculate the height of the Humboldt Falls.

\AnswerLine[][] m
\Marks{2}

\end{subparts}

\item Water flows over the Browne Falls at a rate of 3680 litres per second.
After rain, this rate increases to 9752 litres per second.
Calculate the percentage increase in this rate.

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{3}

\item After rain, water flows over the Sutherland Falls at a rate of 74240 litres per second.
This is an increase of 45\% on the rate before the rain.
Calculate the rate before the rain.

\AnswerLine[][] litres/second
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

$^{\circ}$

tex:

(a) The table shows the amount of time, T minutes, 120 people each spend in a supermarket one Saturday.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Time (T minutes) & Number of people \\
\hline
10 $\leq$ T $<$ 30 & 16 \\
\hline
30 $\leq$ T $<$ 40 & 18 \\
\hline
40 $\leq$ T $<$ 45 & 22 \\
\hline
45 $\leq$ T $<$ 50 & 40 \\
\hline
50 $\leq$ T $<$ 60 & 21 \\
\hline
60 $\leq$ T $<$ 70 & 3 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

 min

\newline

[4]

(d) This histogram shows the amount of time, T minutes, 120 people each spend in the supermarket one
Wednesday.
\newline

Make a comment comparing the distributions of the times for the two days.
\newline

 [1]

\noindent\textbf{[2018 Oct/Nov, 0580/42, Question 9]}\par

\begin{parts}
\item The table shows the amount of time, T minutes, 120 people each spend in a supermarket one Saturday.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Time (T minutes) & Number of people \\
\hline
10 $\leq$ T $<$ 30 & 16 \\
\hline
30 $\leq$ T $<$ 40 & 18 \\
\hline
40 $\leq$ T $<$ 45 & 22 \\
\hline
45 $\leq$ T $<$ 50 & 40 \\
\hline
50 $\leq$ T $<$ 60 & 21 \\
\hline
60 $\leq$ T $<$ 70 & 3 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\begin{subparts}

\Marks{4}

qtype: Rate calculation, unit conversion, volume calculation
tex:
**[2018 Oct/Nov, 0580/42, Question 10]**

(a) The lake behind a dam has an area of 55 hectares.
\newline
When the gates in the dam are open, water flows out at a rate of 75000 litres per second.
\newline

(i) (b) Show that 90 million litres of water flows out in 20 minutes.
\newline

[1]

(c) Beneath the surface, the lake has vertical sides.
\newline
Calculate the drop in the water level of the lake when the gates are open for 20 minutes.
\newline
Give your answer in centimetres.
\newline
[1 hectare = 10$^{4}$ m$^{2}$, 1000 litres = 1 m$^{3}$]

 cm

(d) The cross-section of a gate is a sector of a circle with radius 8.5 m and angle 76°.
\newline
Calculate the perimeter of the sector.
\newline

 m

(e) A solid metal cone has radius 10 cm and height 36 cm.
\newline

(i) (f) Calculate the volume of this cone.
\newline
[The volume, V, of a cone with radius r and height h is V = $\dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^{2}h$.
\newline
]

 cm$^{3}$

(g) The cone is cut, parallel to its base, to give a smaller cone.
\newline

The volume of the smaller cone is half the volume of the original cone.
\newline
The smaller cone is melted down to make two different spheres.
\newline
The ratio of the radii of these two spheres is 1 : 2.
\newline
Calculate the radius of the smaller sphere.
\newline
[The volume, V, of a sphere with radius r is V = $\dfrac{4}{3}\pi r^{3}$.
\newline
]

 cm

content.content:
The lake behind a dam has an area of 55 hectares.
\newline
When the gates in the dam are open, water flows out at a rate of 75000 litres per second.
\newline

Show that 90 million litres of water flows out in 20 minutes.
\newline

[1]

Beneath the surface, the lake has vertical sides.
\newline
Calculate the drop in the water level of the lake when the gates are open for 20 minutes.
\newline
Give your answer in centimetres.
\newline
[1 hectare = 10$^{4}$ m$^{2}$, 1000 litres = 1 m$^{3}$]

The cross-section of a gate is a sector of a circle with radius 8.5 m and angle 76°.
\newline
Calculate the perimeter of the sector.
\newline

A solid metal cone has radius 10 cm and height 36 cm.
\newline

Calculate the volume of this cone.
\newline
[The volume, V, of a cone with radius r and height h is V = $\dfrac{1}{3}\pi r^{2}h$.
\newline
]

The cone is cut, parallel to its base, to give a smaller cone.
\newline

The volume of the smaller cone is half the volume of the original cone.
\newline
The smaller cone is melted down to make two different spheres.
\newline
The ratio of the radii of these two spheres is 1 : 2.
\newline
Calculate the radius of the smaller sphere.
\newline
[The volume, V, of a sphere with radius r is V = $\dfrac{4}{3}\pi r^{3}$.
\newline
]

tex:
**[2018 Oct/Nov, 0580/42, Question 12]**

A box contains 20 packets of potato chips.
\newline
6 packets contain barbecue flavoured chips.
\newline
10 packets contain salt flavoured chips.
\newline
4 packets contain chicken flavoured chips.
\newline

(a) Maria takes two packets at random without replacement.
\newline

(i) (b) Show that the probability that she takes two packets of salt flavoured chips is $\dfrac{9}{38}$.
\newline

[2]

(c) Find the probability that she takes two packets of different flavoured chips.
\newline

(d) Maria takes three packets at random, without replacement, from the 20 packets.
\newline
Find the probability that she takes at least two packets of chicken flavoured chips.
\newline

\newline

Maria takes three packets at random, without replacement, from the 20 packets.
\newline

\newline
